response:
the area of the regular hexagonal community center is calculated by dividing the shape into six equilateral triangles, where each side measures 30 meters, and applying the formula for the area of an equilateral triangle which is the square root of three divided by four times the side squared, resulting in a total area of 1350 times the square root of three square meters, approximately 2338.27 square meters when rounded to two decimal places.

the radius of the circular garden touching the midpoints of the hexagon's sides corresponds to the apothem of the hexagon, which is found by multiplying half the side length by the square root of three, yielding a radius of 15 times the square root of three meters, and the area of this circle is determined by squaring this radius and multiplying by pi, resulting in 675 times pi square meters, approximately 2120.58 square meters when rounded to two decimal places.
